commit.message:
feat: Add complete Map SDK with IMapModule interface

🗺️ Custom Map Development System

Added:
- IMapModule.h: Complete C++ interface for custom maps
  - Map identity (name, author, version)
  - Properties (difficulty, theme, max players)
  - Spawn points, goals, bounds definitions
  - Lifecycle callbacks (OnLoad, OnUnload, etc)
  - Custom game rules support

- ExampleMap.cpp: Full working example implementation
  - Demonstrates all required methods
  - Shows spawn point/goal configuration
  - Ready-to-use template for map creators

- MapSDK/README.md: Comprehensive developer documentation
  - Quick start guide
  - Complete API reference
  - Unity building instructions
  - Troubleshooting guide

Research Documents:
- MAPS_RESEARCH.md: Unity Addressables analysis
  - Asset bundle structure
  - Addressables 2.8.1 architecture
  - Map loading strategy

- MAP_ANALYSIS.md: Extracted assets analysis
  - All 46 golf courses documented
  - Scene structure breakdown
  - Required components identified

Why Native Wins:
✅ Direct IL2CPP hooks (unlike BepInEx)
✅ Can inject Addressables catalogs
✅ Full Unity scene control
✅ First working custom maps for SBG!

Next: Phase 2 IL2CPP integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add social media auto-posting pipeline with Twitter support

- Two-phase architecture: daily content generation + scheduled posting
- SocialMediaOrchestrator generates 5 tweet facts + 1 blog link tweet via OpenAI
- SocialMediaPostingTrigger fires every 4h to post from Cosmos DB queue
- Weighted post selection: lower twitterCount/backlinkCount = higher probability
- New Cosmos container 'social-media-queue' with 48h TTL
- Track twitterCount per post, increment on link tweet posting
- PostRawAsync for pre-formatted content posting
- 6 new unit tests (57 total passing)

commit.message:
fix: Normalize LLM grammar gap IDs to catalog structure IDs

Root cause: post-session consolidation LLM generates free-form structure
IDs ('article_usage', 'verb_conjugation', 'adjective_agreement') that
don't match our curated catalog IDs ('articles_definis_indefinis',
'present_tense', 'adjectifs_accord'). This caused the roadmap to show
'Conversation libre' for most lessons (no theme matches) and the
progress overview to miss most learned structures.

Fix: Added normalize_gap_id() mapping with ~50 known LLM→catalog
mappings. Applied normalization in:
- Planner: review structure selection + introducible computation
- Roadmap: mastery overview + structures in rotation
- Catalog API: structure status enrichment

Result: Roadmap now shows themed lessons (Cuisine, Routine quotidienne)
with matching phrase categories instead of generic conversation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add missing getClientProfile import — fixes blank page on login (#71)

* fix: add missing getClientProfile import — prevents blank page on login

The `getClientProfile` function was called in a useEffect (triggered
when `uid` becomes non-null on login) but was never imported.
This caused a ReferenceError that crashed the React tree → blank page.

commit.message:
ci: book000/templates パターンへの統合 (#2)

ci: book000/templates パターンに合わせてワークフローを整理

- concurrency group に event_name を追加し push と pull_request が
  互いをキャンセルしないよう修正（CI がキャンセルされた根本原因）
- release.yml を削除し、リリース処理を ci.yml に統合
- pull_request_target の closed イベントでリリースを作成する方式に変更
  （workflow_run の信頼性問題を回避）
- calc-version job を追加: PR 中は dry_run=true、マージ時は false
- cpp-build が skipped でも finished-ci が pass するよう修正

feat: MCP client runtime — JSON-RPC 2.0, server manager, registry client

- Add crates/caduceus-mcp to workspace
- types.rs: McpServerConfig, McpTransport (stdio/http), McpToolDef,
  McpResource, McpRegistryEntry, ServerStatus, McpPackage, PackageRuntime
- error.rs: McpError with IO/HTTP/JsonRpc/Config variants
- client.rs: McpClient with connect (stdio process spawn + HTTP),
  JSON-RPC 2.0 send/receive, list_tools, call_tool, list_resources,
  read_resource, MCP initialize handshake
- manager.rs: McpServerManager — add/remove servers, start_all,
  all_tools (deduplicated), call_tool routing, health_check, shutdown_all
- registry.rs: McpRegistryClient — search, get_server, install
  (generates McpServerConfig from registry entry packages)
- 24 tests covering: JSON-RPC serialization, response parsing, tool def
  parsing, config loading, manager add/remove/routing, tool aggregation,
  registry entry parsing, install for npm/python/docker runtimes

commit.message:
Add Basic/Advanced debug toggle with hidden event types

Advanced mode reveals:
- Error traces (FlowActionBadRequest, etc.)
- MCP server errors (connector failures)
- Plan step blocked (content filtering/RAI)
- Variable assignments (topic variables being set)
- Topic/dialog redirects
- Unknown intent events
- GPT answer traces
- Generative answers support data
- Escalation/handoff events

Each event type has distinct color coding and icons.
Events link to messages via replyToId (highlight sync).
Badge shows count of hidden events in basic mode.

commit.message:
[Internal] Tests: Refactors HeadersValidationTests to use async/await instead of sync-over-async (#5770)

`HeadersValidationTests.cs` contained 31 sync-over-async anti-patterns
(30 `.Result`, 1 `.Wait()`) across helper methods and test methods.

## Changes

- **Helper methods → `async Task<T>`**: `CreateDocumentRequest`,
`CreateDocumentScript`, `QueryRequest`, `ReadDatabaseFeedRequest`,
`ReadDocumentFeedRequestAsync`,
`ReadDocumentFeedRequestSinglePartition`, `ReadDocumentRequest`,
`ReadFeedScript` — internal `.Result` replaced with `await`

- **Private helpers → `async Task`**: `ValidatePageSize`,
`ValidateJsonSerializationFormat*`,
`ValidateSupportedSerializationFormats*`,
`SupportedSerializationFormats{Negative,Positive}Cases`,
`ValidateIndexingDirective`, `ValidateEnableScanInQuery`,
`ValidateEnableLowPrecisionOrderBy`, `ValidateEmitVerboseTracesInQuery`,
`ValidateIfNonMatch`

- **Test methods → `async Task`**: All `[TestMethod] public void`
wrappers that call async helpers converted to `async Task`

- **`ValidateGlobalCompltedLSNAndNumberOfReadRegionsHeader`**: Converted
to `async Task`; `.Wait()` in `finally` replaced with `await`

- **`catch` blocks updated**: `catch (Exception ex) { ex.InnerException
as DocumentClientException }` → `catch (DocumentClientException ex)`
since `await` unwraps `AggregateException`

```csharp
// Before
private DocumentServiceResponse ReadDatabaseFeedRequest(DocumentClient client, INameValueCollection headers)
{
    var response = client.ReadFeedAsync(request, null).Result;
    return response;
}

// After
private async Task<DocumentServiceResponse> ReadDatabaseFeedRequest(DocumentClient client, INameValueCollection headers)
{
    return await client.ReadFeedAsync(request, null);
}
```

`ValidateVersionHeader`'s `AggregateException` catch is intentionally
unchanged — the exception originates from the synchronous
`TestCommon.CreateClient()` call, not an awaited path.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: kirankumarkolli <6880899+kirankumarkolli@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Kiran Kumar Kolli <kirankk@microsoft.com>

fix: strip quoted strings before security pattern matching in hook

Prevents false positives when bash command arguments contain blacklisted
keywords (e.g., gh release --notes mentioning 'npm publish').

Uses sed to replace single and double quoted strings with placeholder
before running grep checks. Direct unquoted commands still caught.

Added 15-case false-positive test suite covering all affected roles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix test result parsing: trust parsed counts over exit code

When dotnet test returns non-zero exit code (e.g., one test project
fails to build) but all parsed tests pass (0 failed, 204 passed),
the old logic incorrectly reported failure. This caused PE agents to
waste 3 fix attempts and then remove passing tests.

New logic: when test counts are successfully parsed from output,
trust them over the process exit code. Fall back to exit code only
when no test output can be parsed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
release: v1.0.5 (#38)

* chore: update branching strategy and CI for release workflow

- CONVENTIONS.md: replace branch strategy with release-branch model
  (fix/* → release/X.Y.x → main, long-lived release branches)
- CI: add release/** to push and pull_request triggers
- Add version bump advisory and GitHub Releases policy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both
LimitCreate and LimitUpdate ensures the stored value always maps cleanly
to a whole percentage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: draw continuous average trend line across sparse historical data

Use null instead of omitting the average property so Recharts
connectNulls draws a continuous dashed line through gaps. Also guard
the tooltip against null values to avoid showing misleading \.

Adds backend test documenting _average_series behavior with empty
periods (months with zero expenses are excluded from the average).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: limit Insights transactions to first page with View all link (#27)

Remove infinite scroll from the Insights transaction panel. Only show
the first page (~20 items) and add a View all link that navigates to
/transactions with the current filters applied.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: send category filters in correct schema format when creating limits (#23)

Frontend was sending category_ids (ignored by Pydantic) instead of
filters: [{filter_type, filter_value}]. Also fixed edit form loading
f.id (filter record UUID) instead of f.filter_value (category UUID).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: extend trend line to full period and use 1-based days (#26)

_to_cumulative now accepts period_days so the current series extends
to the end of the month/week even when there are no recent expenses.
get_day_of_period returns 1-based indices so the X-axis starts at
day 1 instead of misleading day 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review findings for trend line changes

- Update docstring and ARCHITECTURE.md to reflect 1-based day index
- Use public get_day_of_period instead of private _resolve_local_date
- Cap period_days as hard limit (ignore timezone-shifted outliers)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: send empty filters array when clearing category filter on edit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: limit progress %, UUID display, filter update, and validation (#30)

- Multiply backend 0-1 progress ratio by 100 in limit-card and home
- Resolve category UUIDs to names via batch query (filter_display_name)
- Show 'Deleted category' instead of raw UUID for removed categories
- Add filters field to LimitUpdate schema with delete-all/re-insert
- Reject filters: null with 422 (explicit over silent)
- Validate category filter_value as UUID at schema boundary
- Guard against empty category_ids producing IN () that matches all
- Extract _resolve_filter_display for clarity over inline ternary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: format files for CI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — type hints, corrupt filter guard, missing tests

- Add type annotations to create_limit and update_limit signatures
- Match nothing (not everything) when all category filter UUIDs are corrupt
- Add tests: invalid UUID rejection, filters=null 422, deleted category fallback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: extend average trend line to full period, weekday labels, dynamic legend (#31)

- Pass current period_days to all historical _to_cumulative calls so
  average series always covers the full period (no early cutoff)
- Add timeframe to SpendingTrendResponse schema so frontend knows mode
- Weekly X-axis shows Mon-Sun instead of day numbers
- Weekly tooltip shows weekday name instead of Day N
- Legend shows 3-week avg vs 3-month avg based on timeframe
- Updated tests for normalized average behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: constrain timeframe type, fix legend labels, remove unused prop

- Backend: Literal type for timeframe in SpendingTrendResponse
- Frontend: SpendingTrendTimeframe union type
- Legend now derives from data.timeframe (works for last_week, ytd too)
- Remove unused periodLabel prop from SpendingTrendChart
- Rename stale test to match updated behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: average series normalization, yearly skip, period labels (#31)

- _average_series divides by total series count (not per-day count)
- Skip 3-year average for ytd (too expensive, not useful)
- Restore periodLabel for current-period display (This/Last week/month)
- Add PERIOD_DISPLAY and AVG_LABEL maps for all timeframes
- Conditionally render average Line and legend only when data exists
- Add test for yearly average skip
- Constrain timeframe to Literal type in schema

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden _average_series with carry-forward for short series

_average_series now fills missing trailing days by carrying forward
each series' last cumulative value. This guarantees the average never
decreases even if a caller passes un-normalized series, preventing
future regressions without relying on caller discipline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: shared period context across Home, Transactions, and Insights (#28)

Add PeriodProvider React context holding this_week/this_month. Home's
toggle writes to context. Transactions and Insights read it as their
default period, with local filter overrides taking precedence.

- Strip cleared filter values before merging so deselecting a chip
  falls back to globalPeriod instead of undefined
- hasFilters computed from local overrides only (not inherited period)
- Insights transaction cap reduced from 20 to 15
- Simplified Insights View all link (context handles period)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: shared period context with localStorage, filter override isolation (#28)

Add PeriodProvider React context (this_week/this_month) persisted in
localStorage. Home toggle writes to context. Transactions and Insights
use it as default period for data queries.

- FilterBar sees localFilters directly (period chip fully interactive)
- queryFilters merges globalPeriod only when no local period is set
- hasFilters computed from local overrides only (no phantom Reset)
- usePeriod throws if called outside PeriodProvider
- Insights transactions capped at 15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Update netlify/functions/campaign-blueprint-create.js

commit.message:
[Internal] Tests: Refactors QueryTests to use async/await instead of sync-over-async (#5774)

`QueryTests.cs` contained 61 sync-over-async anti-patterns (50
`.Result`, 11 `.Wait()`) that risk deadlocks and obscure exceptions via
`AggregateException` wrapping.

## Changes

- **`void` → `async Task`** for test methods: `TestQueryWithPageSize`,
`TestLazyIndexAllTerms`, `TestQueryMetricsHeaders`,
`TestForceQueryScanHeaders`, `TestContinuationLimitHeaders`,
`TestQueryUnicodeDocumentHttpsGateway`
- **`void` → `async Task`** for private helpers: `CleanUp`,
`TestQueryUnicodeDocument`, and the private overloads of
`TestQueryMetricsHeaders`, `TestForceQueryScanHeaders`,
`TestContinuationLimitHeaders`
- **`TestInitialize` / `TestCleanup`** converted to `async Task` to
properly `await CleanUp()`
- **`RetryRateLimiting` → `AsyncRetryRateLimiting`** with async lambdas
wherever `.Result` appeared inside sync retry wrappers
- **`AggregateException` catch blocks** in
`TestContinuationLimitHeaders` replaced with `DocumentClientException` —
the unwrapped type thrown by `await`
- **`.Wait()` in already-async methods**
(`TestQueryForRoutingMapSanity`, `TestQueryParallelExecution`,
`TestReadFeedParallelQuery`) replaced with `await`

### Before / After

```csharp
// Before
Database database = TestCommon.RetryRateLimiting<Database>(() =>
    this.client.CreateDatabaseAsync(...).Result.Resource);

catch (AggregateException e) when (e.InnerException is DocumentClientException ex) { ... }

// After
Database database = await TestCommon.AsyncRetryRateLimiting<Database>(async () =>
    (await this.client.CreateDatabaseAsync(...)).Resource);

catch (DocumentClientException ex) { ... }
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: kirankumarkolli <6880899+kirankumarkolli@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Kiran Kumar Kolli <kirankk@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
Extract media3-timeshift library module

Extract CircularByteBuffer, TimeShiftDataSource, and PlaybackMode from
app into a reusable Android library at libs/media3-timeshift under
package com.cascadiacollections.android.media3.timeshift.

Changes:
- Add sir.android.lib convention plugin (build-logic)
- Add android-library and media3-datasource to version catalog
- Create libs/media3-timeshift module with README, consumer ProGuard
  rules, and comprehensive KDoc
- Parameterize TimeShiftDataSource threadName and chunkSize
- Change visibility from internal to public for library consumers
- Migrate 26 tests from app to library module
- Update app to depend on :libs:media3-timeshift
- Add docs/library-extraction-plan.md for Phase 2/3 roadmap

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add complete 2024 AFL home-and-away season fixture

Add afl_simulation/afl_fixture_2024.py containing the full 2024 AFL
fixture from Opening Round (Round 0) through Round 24 (207 matches).

The module provides:
- Fixture and Round dataclasses for structured match data
- get_fixture() to retrieve the complete fixture
- get_round() to retrieve a specific round by number

commit.message:
Add name/length metadata to String prototype/constructor methods

Wrap String prototype methods and String.fromCharCode with
make_native_fn() instead of bare VmNativeFunction, so they
expose correct .name and .length per the ES spec.

commit.message:
feat: Redesigned roadmap with phrases, progress overview, 3-tab layout

Roadmap backend:
- ProgressOverview: sessions, vocab count, phrases available, grammar stats
- PhraseCategoryProgress: 13 phrase categories with counts and difficulty
- PlannedLesson now includes phrase_categories for each lesson
- Roadmap includes phrase bank data alongside grammar mastery

RoadmapPanel redesigned with 3 tabs:
- Overview: stats grid (sessions/vocab/phrases/grammar), grammar progress
  bar with legend, next lesson card with tap-to-start
- Lessons: timeline cards showing both phrases and grammar per lesson,
  NEW tags for new structures
- Skills: phrases by situation (13 categories with counts), grammar
  structures in compact grouped-by-status layout

Phrases are now first-class alongside grammar — lesson cards show
phrase categories (e.g. 'Commander au restaurant') before grammar names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
